latest.title: Total Voting Rights and Share Capital
latest.content:
Today 12:04
RNS Number : 9603U
Iomart Group PLC
02 March 2026
iomart Group plc
("iomart" or the "Company")
Total Voting Rights and Share Capital
iomart makes the following announcement in accordance with Rule 5.6.1 of the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
As at 28 February 2026, the total number of Ordinary Shares of 1 pence each of iomart Group plc is 113,728,770. There are 140,773 Ordinary Shares in the iomart Group plc Employee Benefit Trust and the Company holds none of its Ordinary Shares in treasury.
Therefore, the total number of voting rights in iomart Group plc is 113,587,997.
The above figure of 113,587,997 shares may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, iomart Group plc, under the Disclosures Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Enquiries:
iomart Group plc
Julie Brown
Company Secretary
investorrelations@iomart.com

latest.title: Directorate Change and Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4998S
Iomart Group PLC
11 February 2026
11 February 2026
The information contained within this announcement is deemed to constitute inside information as stipulated under the retained EU law version of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 (the "UK MAR") which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018. The information is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of the UK MAR. Upon the publication of this announcement, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
iomart Group plc
("iomart" or the "Group" or the "Company")
Directorate Change and Trading Update
Directorate Change
iomart Group plc (AIM: IOM), the
secure cloud services company
,
announces that Scott Cunningham, Chief Financial Officer, has notified the Board of his intention to step down from his role to take up a new opportunity in a private company outside of the IT sector. To ensure a smooth transition, Scott will continue in his role and work closely with Richard Last, Executive Chair, and the wider leadership team through the completion of the March year‑end reporting cycle. It is expected that Scott will leave the Board and the Company in June 2026.
Scott has served iomart with dedication for over seven years, playing a significant leadership role across the Group's financial, operational and strategic development. The Board would like to express its thanks to Scott for his contribution during a period of considerable organisational evolution and transformation. The process to appoint Scott's successor will commence immediately.
Richard Last, Executive Chair of iomart Group plc, commented:
"On behalf of the Board and the wider iomart team, I wish to thank Scott for his significant commitment and contribution to iomart over the last seven years and wish him every success in his new role."
Trading Performance
The Group experienced a softening in trading during December and January, including an increase in customer churn compared to the earlier part of the financial year, particularly in certain high margin areas. Growth, albeit lower than expected, has been achieved in Azure, security and Microsoft 365 where margins are traditionally lower.
Net order bookings are expected to be positive for the financial year, and the sales pipeline is encouraging.
The Group continues its cost optimisation and efficiency program, with annualised savings of over £5.0m now having been achieved. We continue to pursue further efficiency opportunities which will generate further profit benefits, operational efficiencies and service quality improvements.
Overall, for the year to 31 March 2026, the Board now expects Group revenue to be broadly in line with, and EBITDA to be just below, the lower end of current market expectations.
Cash performance and capital expenditure discipline remain strong, and the Group continues to operate within its current bank facilities.
Note: Company compiled range is based on known sell-side analyst estimates. The latest known sell-side analyst estimates for the full year ended 31 March 2026 are:
·
Revenue in the range of £157.6m to £159.1m;
·
Adjusted EBITDA
(1)
in the range of £27.7m to £28.5m; and
·
Adjusted loss before tax
(2
in the range of £(2.1)m to £(1.0)m
·
Net Debt in the range of £98m to £107m
(1)
adjusted EBITDA means earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, amortisation, share based payment charges, forex gains or losses on long term cash flow hedges, acquisition related costs and non-recurring items.
(2)
adjusted loss before tax means profits before, tax, share based payment charges, amortisation of acquired intangibles, forex gains or losses on long term cash flow hedges, acquisition related costs and non-recurring items.
The person responsible for releasing this announcement on behalf of the Company is Richard Last, Executive Chair.
For further information:
iomart Group plc
Tel: 0141 931 6400
Richard Last, Executive Chair
Investec Bank PLC (Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Tel: 020 7597 4000
Patrick Robb, Virginia Bull
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: 020 3405 0205
Caroline Forde, Hilary Buchanan,
Louisa El-Ahwal
About iomart Group plc
Iomart Group plc (AIM: IOM) is one of the UK's leading providers of secure cloud managed services, simplifying the complexities of modern technology for businesses, with the majority of Group revenue derived from the UK. Our team of 600+ deliver cutting-edge solutions in cloud infrastructure, modern workplace management, and managed security services that enable our customers to innovate, protect, and scale their businesses.
We hold one of the UK's most extensive sets of Microsoft credentials, including Azure Expert MSP, Eight Solution Designations, 15 Advanced Specialisations including the new Copilot award and membership in Microsoft's Intelligent Security Association (MISA). As well as being a top-tier Broadcom Pinnacle Partner for VMware Cloud and Elite MSP Partner for Commvault. Which means we can bring the latest technologies in hybrid cloud, data protection, and cyber resiliency to meet the evolving needs of our customer
For further information about the Group, please visit
www.iomart.com

latest.title: Change of Auditor
latest.content:
2 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7727J
Iomart Group PLC
02 December 2025
2 December 2025
Iomart Group plc
("Iomart", the "Group" or the "Company")
Change of Auditor
Iomart (AIM:IOM), the secure cloud services company, is pleased to announce that, following the completion of a competitive tender process, it has appointed Grant Thornton UK LLP ("Grant Thornton") as its new auditor with effect from 1 December 2025 for the year ending 31 March 2026.
The appointment of Grant Thornton as auditor for the following financial year will be subject to approval by shareholders at the 2026 Annual General Meeting of the Company.
At the Board's request, the Company's previous auditors, Deloitte LLP ("Deloitte"), who were the Company's auditors for 6 years, have resigned. In accordance with section 519 of the Companies Act 2006, Deloitte has provided the Company with the required statement of reasons, together with circumstances in connection with, its resignation as auditor. This statement is available, along with our explanatory letter to shareholders, on the Group's website at
Results & Reports | Financials & Presentations | Iomart
. Both documents are being sent to shareholders in accordance with statutory timescales.
For further information:

company_30s.what_they_do: IOM Ltd supplies AI‑powered analytics tools that help mid‑size businesses streamline supply‑chain and customer‑experience processes.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company taps into the growing demand for data‑driven decision making. Its software can reduce operating costs by 10‑15 % for firms that adopt it, giving it a clear niche in the broader digital‑transformation market.
company_30s.current_state: IOM is currently in a restructuring phase after a CEO exit and a new CFO announcement. The firm is still burning cash while it works on a new product roadmap. Its shares trade at a fraction of the valuation that competitors command.

rns_narrative: IOM has been busy reshuffling its board and management team, signalling a leadership reset.
The company has made several changes to its share capital and director holdings, which suggests a move to improve governance and attract fresh capital.
Recent filings show a shift to a new brand name, ‘Atech’, hinting at a product‑line refresh that could attract new customers.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet uncertainty – the community is largely silent, but a few voices are warning that the company may be losing its core business.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There are no clear bullish voices; the absence of chatter is actually a contrarian signal that the market has not yet seen the potential for a rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue that the leadership vacuum and weak earnings forecasts point to a continuing slide. They fear the company may be unable to turn around its cash burn.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat over the past week. The lack of movement suggests the market is still waiting for a catalyst before deciding.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search interest – a dead trend that gives the stock a surprise edge if a catalyst appears.

near_term_catalysts: The next earnings release could reveal a better‑than‑expected operating margin if the new product line starts generating revenue.
A formal announcement of a new CEO or a partnership with a larger tech firm could lift investor confidence.
If the company initiates a modest share buyback or raises a small equity round, it would signal confidence in the business.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike of at least 1.5‑times the 20‑day average, a price break above the 20‑day moving average, and a positive earnings surprise of 15 % or more.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of a crash‑recovery cycle – it has not yet reached a clear bottom. A confirmation of improved fundamentals is needed before a sustained bounce can occur.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: IOM’s price is down more than 70 % from its recent high. If the company’s new product line starts pulling in revenue, the stock could climb to about 50 % of its pre‑crash level, delivering roughly a 200 % upside. The deep crash gives the most asymmetric payoff potential.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the leadership changes fail to reverse the cash burn and the company misses earnings expectations, the stock could fall another 10–15 % before stabilising. The worst case is a further 10 % slide to the current level, which is already a significant loss from the peak.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is about 200 % versus a downside of roughly 70 %. The risk‑reward ratio is roughly 2.9:1, a classic high‑asymmetry contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a positive earnings announcement or a confirmed new CEO. Size the position so that a 20 % drop from entry would trigger a stop. Use a trailing stop at 10 % to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike of 1.5× the 20‑day average and a price break above the 20‑day moving average. Only then consider a small entry to test the new support.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risk has surfaced; the only real danger would be a regulatory investigation or a forced delisting, which are currently not evident.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IOM has plunged deep, but a fresh leadership team and a potential product refresh create a high‑asymmetry bounce if the company can turn its cash burn into revenue.
